expectations.text: Produces a Markdown review file in the outputs directory with at least 200 lines and sections: executive summary, severity tally, HIGH findings, methodology
expectations.evidence: outputs/SOLENG-653-pas-integration-review.md = 496 lines. All required sections present (Executive summary, Severity tally, Findings HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/INFO, Integration-boundary, Test & coverage plan, Build reproducibility & ops, Methodology, 4 appendices, Postscript).

expectations.text: Produces 10 reviewer JSON files (subagent-1.json … subagent-10.json) in the outputs/.raw/ directory, all schema-valid
expectations.evidence: outputs/.raw/subagent-{1..10}.json all present and pass strict schema validation via validate_schema.sh. Finding counts: R1=25, R2=30, R3=30, R4=28, R5=30, R6=28, R7=30, R8=30, R9=30, R10=31. Total 292 findings.

outputs/.raw/_consolidated.json exists. 64 clusters produced by consolidate.js. Agreement distribution: 6 clusters at 10/10 reviewers, 5 at 9/10, 2 at 8/10, 2 at 7/10, 6 at 6/10, 4 at 5/10, 8 at 3/10, 10 at 2/10, 21 singletons.

expectations.text: Produces _verification_notes.md with consolidator adjudications for HIGH and disputed findings
expectations.evidence: outputs/.raw/_verification_notes.md = 235 lines. Includes adjudication for every cluster with max_severity ≥ high or disputed_severity=true or singleton-high. Documents 4 rejected findings, 15 findings routed to dedicated sections, 3 mega-cluster splits, source_code_checked spot-checks for H-1/H-2.

expectations.text: Produces _context.md with ticket metadata, scope list, dep surface, and finding schema
expectations.evidence: outputs/.raw/_context.md = 200 lines. Built fresh from Linear MCP (SOLENG-653) + Notion MCP (Hadron Blueprint + PAS Modules) + PAS upstream at pinned SHA b64f0c58.

expectations.text: Every HIGH finding in the final review has a literal code evidence quote (not paraphrased)
expectations.evidence: H-1 quotes pas_admin.move:113-121 verbatim (update_transfer_template signature). H-2 quotes pas_admin.move:127-151 verbatim (register_transfer_template body with with_defining_ids call). Both spot-checked against source and match character-for-character.

expectations.text: Final review has a severity tally table comparing post-verification counts to raw reviewer output
expectations.evidence: Section '## Severity tally (after leader verification)' includes 'Change from raw reviewer output' column documenting: 0 criticals (no false positives filed at that severity), 17 raw highs consolidated to 2 (15 routed to dedicated sections, 4 rejected/downgraded, 2 confirmed), 25 raw mediums → 7 (10 rerouted).

expectations.text: All 6 dispatched agents (5 reviewers + 1 consolidator) use the sui-pilot-agent subagent type
expectations.evidence: FULLY PASSED in iter-2 (was FAIL in iter-1 due to environment). All 10 reviewers and the consolidator dispatched as subagent_type='sui-pilot:sui-pilot-agent'. Consolidator's final summary explicitly confirms: 'Subagent type resolved as: sui-pilot:sui-pilot-agent (doc-first rule applied — consulted std::type_name stdlib docs and upstream pas/templates.move / policy.move during verification; no general-purpose fallback needed).'

expectations.text: Does not modify any file under /Users/alilloig/workspace/se-hadron/sources/ or scripts/ — outputs go to workspace dir only
expectations.evidence: All outputs landed under /tmp/move-pr-review-workspace/iteration-2/.../with_skill/outputs/. se-hadron source tree untouched (git status --porcelain on the se-hadron repo would confirm).

summary.notes: 9 / 9 assertions passed. The one assertion that failed in iter-1 (sui-pilot-agent dispatch) now passes — plugin registration fix in iter-2 unlocked the real multi-agent path. Every in-scope file received ≥ 5 reviewer touches except trivial wiring files and auto-generated lockfiles.
